etymology.description: semantic 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 畐(畐) (fú) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character 逼(bī, "force; compel") combines the semantic element 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement"), suggesting motion or advancement, with the phonetic element 畐(畐) (fú), which provides the sound. Together, these convey the concept of forcing or compelling something to move or advance.

examples.chinese: 他常常逼我做不想做的事(他常常逼我做不想做的事)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chángcháng bī wǒ zuò bù xiǎng zuò de shì.
examples.english: He often forces me to do things I don't want to do.

examples.chinese: 老板逼员工加班(老闆逼員工加班)，引起了很多不满(引起了很多不滿)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎobǎn bī yuángōng jiābān, yǐnqǐle hěnduō bùmǎn.
examples.english: The boss forces employees to work overtime, causing a lot of dissatisfaction.

examples.chinese: 压力感到越逼迫(壓力感到越逼迫)，我越想放弃(我越想放棄)。
examples.pinyin: Yālì gǎndào yuè bīpò, wǒ yuè xiǎng fàngqì.
examples.english: The more pressured I feel, the more I want to give up.
